Working deck scaffold

Cost-to-income: turning Barclays’ ambition into a small set of data-led value cases

A workshop structure that anchors brainstorming in peer gaps, Barclays’ targets, and measurable delivery levers.

What we will align

The “few things” that move cost-to-income with pace and credibility.

How we will do it

Peer outcomes → gap → focus areas → “X → Y → £Z” value cases.

What we will leave with

2–3 value cases, owners, and a 30–60 day validation plan.

Core message

“Simpler” must become measurable productivity to hit low-50s cost-to-income.

Prepared for internal stakeholder alignment
Barclays | cost-to-income

Barclays has made cost-to-income the hinge metric for delivering higher returns

Barclays’ published trajectory links returns to an explicit efficiency pathway: a move from ~61% cost:income toward the low-50s by 2028, underpinned by ~£2bn of cost efficiency savings (2026–2028).

>12% → >14%
Statutory RoTE target: 2026 → 2028
~61% → low-50s%
Cost:income path: 2025 → 2028
~£2bn
Cost efficiency savings across 2026–2028
c.£31bn
Income guidance (scale anchor)

Workshop rule

Every idea must state: what changes (operational KPI), how it moves cost:income, and how it becomes £ run-rate.

Target pathway (illustrative)

2025 → 2026 → 2028
2025

Cost:income ~61%

Baseline for delivery conversation

2026

High 50s%

Guidance milestone

2028

Low 50s%

End-state target

Focus: translate “simpler” initiatives into measurable, auditable productivity and cost takeout.

Source: Barclays Progress and Targets Update (Feb 2026).

Slide intent: align on the “why now” and the outcome metrics
2

Peer outcomes show a clear efficiency frontier (and the size of the gap)

We don’t need to claim we know peer programmes. Their outcomes still tell us what’s possible and where the biggest structural differences sit.

What to notice

  • Barclays’ cost:income is improving, but remains above the best operators.
  • Cost composition suggests a material staff productivity gap versus leaders.

Working question

If leaders operate at materially lower ratios, what must be true about their operating model (standardisation, automation, throughput per colleague)?

Cost:income ratio (FY-12/2025)

Selected peers

Note: values from peer comparison pack (FY-12/2025).

Staff cost / income ratio (FY-12/2025)

Where the gap looks structural
Slide intent: quantify the gap and make it discussable
3

Where the cost-to-income opportunity sits: focus where earnings and inefficiency intersect

To move group cost:income, we should prioritise the divisions that are both material to earnings and have headroom to improve.

Headline from Barclays mix

  • Investment Bank is ~45% of operating income and runs at ~62% cost:income.
  • Barclays UK is ~30% of operating income and runs at ~56% cost:income.
  • PBWM is small (~5%) but high cost:income (~73%)—a “simplicity credibility” lever.

Working question

If we can only pursue 2–3 value cases first, which combination best moves group cost:income while reinforcing “simpler, better, more balanced”?

Divisions: mix vs cost:income (FY-12/2025)

Bubble size ≈ operating income share

Use: prioritisation lens (not a financial model).

Slide intent: focus the room on the biggest levers
4

Barclays’ blueprint is explicit: simplify platforms, standardise data, embed AI for productivity

Barclays framing (what changes)

Simpler: digitally driven business, standardised organisation, resilient operations.

Acceleration levers: modernise infrastructure, standardise data & dev platforms, embed AI.

Concrete examples Barclays uses

  • Fewer trading book sub-ledgers (11 → 3) to lower costs and speed reporting.
  • Fewer risk/finance tech applications (80+ → <40) to improve data quality and risk management.
  • Support functions are the centre of gravity (~75% of employees).

What this implies for cost-to-income

The path to low-50s requires converting “simpler” into repeatable productivity: fewer platforms, fewer handoffs, and measurable throughput gains.

89% → >90%
Cloud adoption momentum (2025 → 2026 target)
~70% → 100%
Core data on standardised platform (2025 → 2028)
25×
Faster model deployment (example benchmark)
~£2bn
Efficiency savings across 2026–2028

These proof points are useful because they can be tied to measurable operational KPIs and benefit tracking.

Slide intent: align on Barclays’ stated “how”
5

Data-led focus zones to move cost-to-income (and the kind of work it implies)

These zones are designed to steer brainstorming toward “where the gap is” and away from generic transformation ideas.

Support-function productivity

Biggest cost lever

Staff-cost ratio is structurally higher than leaders; Barclays also flags support functions as ~75% of workforce.

Example levers: AI-enabled operations, workflow automation, controls-by-design, benefit tracking.

Finance & risk simplification

On Barclays’ critical path

Barclays explicitly ties app reduction and sub-ledger consolidation to lower costs and faster reporting.

Example levers: platform rationalisation, data standardisation, reporting cycle-time reduction.

Investment Bank efficiency

Largest earnings pool

IB is ~45% of operating income and ~62% cost:income—small improvements can have outsized group impact.

Example levers: front-to-back breaks reduction, shared data/control fabric, process standardisation.

Barclays UK cost-to-serve

Operating leverage

BUK is ~30% of operating income; Barclays cites service model redesign, tech rationalisation, and footprint optimisation.

Example levers: assisted-to-digital shift, servicing redesign, platform simplification.

Slide intent: create structured brainstorm lanes
6

Translate peer gaps into “moves” the bank can execute and measure

This is the core “conversation engine”: gap → move → operational KPI → financial bridge.

How to use this slide

Ask: “Which two moves would we bet on for 12–24 month cost:income impact, and what must be true to deliver them?”

Outcome → move → measure

Workshop table
Peer outcome gap Likely structural move Measure (operational)
Staff cost ratio higher than leaders Standardise + automate support-function value streams Throughput/colleague, STP%, cycle time, control exceptions
Higher overall cost:income Simplify platforms and eliminate handoffs/rework Apps retired, breaks reduced, time-to-change, reliability
Cost takeout credibility risk (benefit leakage) Build “benefit proof” discipline (tracking + controls) Run-rate confirmed, leakage %, auditability of savings

Keep content visible and static (no hidden panels) for PPT conversion.

Slide intent: make “what peers do” concrete without over-claiming
7

Value equation: make “doing X” translate into “£Z run-rate”

1pp cost:income ≈ £0.31bn
Using income guidance of ~£31bn as a scale anchor

Use in the room

  • Size ideas quickly (directional) to prioritise discussion.
  • Force every idea to state the operational KPI that drives the £ outcome.
  • Track benefits as run-rate, not activity.

Note: directional sizing for prioritisation, not a business case.

Example “X → Y → £Z” translations

Starter set
Action (X) Operational KPI (Y) Value (Z)
Remove manual handoffs in finance/risk Close/reporting cycle time ↓; exception rate ↓ ~1pp ≈ £0.31bn
Support-function automation at scale Throughput/colleague ↑; STP% ↑ ~2pp ≈ £0.62bn
Prove benefits (reduce leakage) Run-rate confirmed; leakage% ↓ Confidence uplift
Barclays target context

Underpinned by ~£2bn efficiency savings (2026–2028).

£2bn

Anchor: income guidance and cost efficiency targets are from Barclays’ published update.

Slide intent: keep brainstorming grounded in measurable economics
8

Close: agree 2–3 value cases and a 30–60 day validation plan

Decision outputs

  • Two to three prioritised value cases linked to cost:income.
  • For each: KPI baseline, target movement, and benefit bridge.
  • Owners and dependencies (data, platforms, process, controls).

Suggested “lighthouse” approach

Start with one end-to-end value stream where simplification + automation can show a measurable cycle-time and cost impact quickly, then scale the pattern.

Value case template (fill live)

One slide per value case in the next iteration

Value case name

Owner

KPI moved

e.g., cost:income (pp), cycle time, STP%

Scope

division + function + value stream

Change required

platform / process / controls / people

30–60 day plan

baseline, prototype, benefits proof

Next: convert this HTML scaffold into a PPT-ready narrative deck.

Slide intent: end with commitment, not a list
9
